project_type: Next.js 16.2.4 health/medication reminder app with React 19
test_framework: No test framework configured (no tests directory or test scripts)
verification_instructions: 1. Run npm run build to ensure project compiles. 2. Run npm run lint to check for errors. 3. Verify no references to better-sqlite3 remain in lib/ or app/ code.
environment_constraints: OPEN_INTERNET - full network access. Node.js 22 pre-installed.
contribution_requirements: AGENTS.md: This is NOT the Next.js you know - read node_modules/next/dist/docs/ before writing code. Heed deprecation notices.
key_patterns: Singleton pattern in lib/db.ts exports getDb(). Server actions in lib/actions.ts use 'use server' directive and call getDb(). API routes in app/api/ also call getDb(). page.tsx is a server component that directly uses getDb(). Client components use server actions via useTransition. All data is for user_id=1 (demo single-user app). Only today's data matters - water resets daily, medication taken status is by date.
directory_structure: lib/ - shared utilities (db, actions, types, timezone). app/ - Next.js app router pages and API routes. app/components/ - client components (MedicationChecklist, WaterTracker, AddMedicationForm). app/api/ - REST API routes for medications, water, and reminders.
